name: Francis Center
description: Free clothing, emergency food, and hygiene items. Visits limited to once per month (or weekly if unhoused). Clothing hours: Mon.-Thurs. 10 a.m.-1:30 p.m. Food pantry: Mon.-Thurs. 10 a.m.-2 p.m. Bus: 72. www.franciscenterpdx.org
req: Limited visits per time period — call for current policy

name: 13 Salmon Shower Project
description: Free showers, rest, warm drinks, snacks, and clean clothing. Safe, non-judgmental "Do No Harm Zone." Opened Jan 2024, added second day per week in 2025. At First Unitarian Portland.

name: Behavioral Health Resource Center
description: Tickets are required for entry into the day center. Get tickets at the referral van on the corner of NW Broadway and Glisan from 6 a.m.-1 p.m. daily. Services offered: showers, laundry, restrooms, computers, peer support. Hours: 8 a.m.-8 p.m. daily. CCC-Clean Start Day Storage 435 NW Glisan St. Portland, OR 97209 Drop off belongings and pick up same day. Must fit in a 32-gal- lon storage container. Property will be held for 30 days. Funded by the City of Portland and operated by Central City Concern’s Clean Start program. Hours: Mon.-Sun. 7 a.m.-8 p.m. (except holidays). Max: Red, Blue; Bus: 4, 8, 16, 35, 40

name: CityTeam Portland - Night Strike
description: Under the Burnside Bridge Clothing, blankets, dinner, haircuts and community. Hours: Thurs. 7:30-9 p.m.

name: Cultivate Initiatives
description: Hot showers, clothing, and hygiene products available Mon. and Fri. 10:30 a.m.-3 p.m. Tues., Wed., Thurs. 9.30 a.m.-2 p.m. Check website for additional locations.

name: Hygiene4All
description: Under the Morrison Bridge (Corner of MLK and Belmont St.) Showers, toilets, trash drop-off, laundry exchange, electronics charging station and physical and mental health first aid that is designed, guided and operated by and for unsheltered commu- nity members. Bus tickets and navigation assistance to housing, health, mental health and SUDS care providers. Medical care from Portland Street Medicine offered twice a month. Hours: Sun. 3-7 p.m. Tues. and Thurs. 3-9 p.m

name: JOIN Day Space
description: Mail service. Restrooms, phone charging, internet access, show- ers including ADA/family shower, hygiene items and referral ser- vices. First come, first serve. Many offered on time-barter basis. Weekly/bi-weekly partner services include, Mobile Medical Clin- ic, CCC Employment and CHAT team hours. Hours: Mon., Wed., Fri., Sat., Sun. 9 a.m-5 p.m. and Tues. and Thurs. 10 a.m.-4 p.m. NW Glisan Oasis Day Center 599 NW 6th Ave. Portland, OR 97209 Outdoor space where people can rest, get support navigating to resources, access basic hygiene and other services. Has show- ers, bathrooms, charging stations for personal electronics, and wifi. Hours: 6 a.m.-10 p.m. Daily

name: Rahab’s Sisters
description: Women, trans and non-binary guests are invited inside for re- freshments, connection and activities. Clothing, harm reduction and hygiene supplies are available. Hours: Wed. 2-4 p.m. Thur. 1-4 p.m. Fri. 5-8 p.m

name: Rose Haven
description: Clothing, showers, diapers, hygiene, mail, device charging, pet food, and outdoor supplies for women, children, and marginalized genders. Nutritious daily meals. Advocacy and emotional support. First-come first-served; intakes daily. Morning: Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-noon. Afternoon: Tues./Thurs. 1-4 p.m. Family hours (registered minor children): Wed. 1-4 p.m. Bus: 77; MAX Red, Blue, Streetcar. 1740 NW Glisan St.

name: St. Andre Bessette
description: Emergency clothing once a month; hygiene kits once a week. Emergency groceries (first 4 in line) once a month. Hot meal Fri. 6:30-7:30 p.m. Sack lunch Tues./Wed./Thurs. 9:30-10:30 a.m. Free movies Tues./Thurs. 2-4 p.m. Barber Thurs. 11:30 a.m.-1:30 p.m. Line up at side door on 6th Ave. 601 W Burnside St.

name: Sunnyside Shower Project
description: Free showers, clean clothing, laundry cards, toiletries, and connections to housing/healthcare/recovery. Volunteer-run mutual aid, SE Portland. Three days/week. Received $40K OCF grant in 2025. Low-barrier, no ID required.
hours: Tue, Thu, Sat afternoons — see website for exact times

name: TPI - Bud Clark Commons
description: Restrooms, shower, laundry, mail, clothing, nutrition resources, locker, ID support, and shelter referrals. Sign up at front desk. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8:30 a.m.-7 p.m.; Sat.-Sun. 8:30 a.m.-3:30 p.m. Mail: Mon.-Fri. 8:30-10:30 a.m. & 12:30-3:30 p.m.; Sat. 1-3:30 p.m. Bus: 17, 33; MAX Green, Yellow. 650 NW Irving St.

name: Friendly House
description: Care coordination for adults aged 60 and over. Fill out intake form online at fhpdx.org or call for referral.
req: Serves adults 60+ and/or people with disabilities
Disability documentation may be required for some services

name: Maybelle Center
description: Promotes social health for adults facing the stigmas of mental health and financial poverty. Call or fill out form at maybellecen- ter.org/become-a-member/ to become a member. Must be currently housed, 18 years or older, and live in the downtown Portland area or be able access the Center on 6th and Couch in Old Town
req: Serves adults 60+ and/or people with disabilities
Disability documentation may be required for some services

name: ADRC of Oregon – Aging & Disability Resource Connection
description: Free helpline providing information, assistance, and referrals for adults 60+, people with disabilities of all ages, veterans, and caregivers. Connect to local services including in-home care, transportation, housing, legal aid, and benefits. Available 24/7 statewide.
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member

name: Senior Loneliness Helpline
description: Available 24 hours. Supports aging adults who are experiencing loneliness, isolation, depression or anxiety. Call to speak with confidentiality to a Senior Loneliness specialist. Staffed by clini- cians. Available 24 hours. www.seniorlonelinessline.org
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: Social Security Administration 1-800-772-1213
description: People with disabilities may qualify for monthly benefits from the federal government. Apply online, by phone, or in person. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-4 p.m

name: Bradley Angle
description: Gender-inclusive, confidentially located emergency shelter for single survivors and families fleeing domestic violence. The Bradley Angle Resource Center provides culturally specific pro- grams for Black/African American and LGBTQIA+ communities, housing and financial assistance, financial education, counsel- ing, support groups and youth programs. www.bradleyangle.org
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: Call to Safety
description: Support for all people experiencing harm due to domestic and sexual violence, sexual assault, stalking and sex trafficking. Provides confidential advocacy services to all callers, including a 24/7 crisis line, 24/7 sexual assault crisis response to hospi- tals, case management services and community engagement. Specialized services for sex workers and persons experiencing houselessness caused by domestic and/or sexual violence. All services free of cost. www.calltosafety.org
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: IRCO-RIFS Program
description: Works with survivors of trafficking, domestic and/or sexual violence. 24-hour line. Calls forwarded to Call to Safety after business hours. NAYA Family Center-Healing Circle Program 503-288-8177 ext. 339 An array of services for survivors of domestic violence and sexu- al assault and youth experiencing sex trafficking including safety planning, housing stabilization, crisis intervention, advocacy and educational services. The Healing Circle works to reinforce the fact that domestic violence is not, nor ever has been, a traditional Native American value. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-6 p.m
req: Free services — open to all genders unless specified

name: Providence S.A.F.E. Center
address: 4400 NE Halsey St. Bldg. 1 Ste. 286 Portland, OR 97213
description: Medical clinic providing care to individuals who have experi- enced sexual, physical or psychological violence. We provide medical care and follow-up, STI and pregnancy preventative medication, safety planning, forensic photography, and forensic evidence collection. You do not need to report to police or be insured to receive care. Medical-forensic services are provided 24/7, please call for an appointment (same day appointments accepted).
req: Free services — open to all genders unless specified

name: Proyecto UNICA
description: El Programa Hispano Catolico-En Español. 24-hour crisis line. Provides bilingual and bicultural services for Spanish speaking survivors of domestic and/or sexual violence. Provides support, advocacy and opportunity for self-empowerment, enabling survi- vors to exercise free and informed life choices
req: Free services — open to all genders unless specified

name: Raphael House of Portland
description: Emergency shelter for single survivors and families fleeing intimate partner abuse. Advocacy for housing, employment, ed- ucation and emotional support. Safety and Stabilization Assess- ment (SSA) from The Gateway Center required. www.raphaelhouse.com
req: Free services — open to all genders unless specified

name: Slavic Oregon Social Services
description: Russian and Ukranian language advocacy, support and case management for survivors of domestic and sexual violence. Call for an appointment. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8:30 a.m.-5 p.m. After hours calls will be forwarded to the Call to Safety crisis line
req: Free services — open to all genders unless specified

name: People over 65 can receive assistance with restraining orders
description: not related to intimate partner violence. Services available in many languages. Call for services. Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m-4 p.m. The Salvation Army - West Women’s and Children’s Shelter 503-731-3900 Emergency shelter for survivors of domestic violence with a wide range of supportive services for clients who apply for shelter. Services include advocacy, housing assistance and food. Call for intake
req: Free services — open to all genders unless specified

name: VOA Oregon Home Free – Emergency DV Services
description: offers crisis support, safety plan and access to motel vouch- ers when available for survivors fleeing domestic and sexual violence. Support with Safety and Stabilization Assessments as capacity allows for referrals into Domestic and Sexual Violence Shelters and Housing Programs. Spanish/English Advocacy, and access to interpretation services. This line forwards to Linea Unica after hours. For Restraining Order Services, call 503-802- 0506 (Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-2 p.m.)
req: Free services — open to all genders unless specified

name: YWCA of Greater Portland
description: Culturally responsive support and housing-related advocacy services for survivors. For one-on-one support email GetServices@ywcapdx.org
req: Free services — open to all genders unless specified

name: CCC - Employment Access Center
description: Accepting referrals for Central City Concern clients and individ- uals enrolled in specific programs. No walk-ins. Call for more information. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-5 p.m. Worksource Express Center in basement is open to walk-ins. Worksource Express Hours: Mon. 8:30 a.m.-noon Tues. 9:30 a.m.-4:30 p.m. Thurs. 8:30 a.m.- 4:30 p.m.
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Central Portland Vocational Rehab Dept.
description: Call to make intake appointment. Vocational rehabilitation, job skills training and job placement for people with disabilities. Hours: Mon.-Fri.8 a.m.-5 p.m. Bus: 17, 70, 77

name: Constructing Hope
description: Pre-apprenticeship training program offers hands-on construc- tion practice, educational training and skill certifications for low-income men and women and those with a legal history. Real world experience. Facilitates permanent job placement for grad- uates. Summer program for ages 16-19. Bus: 6, 72

name: Dress for Success
description: Provides work clothing, job preparation, career counseling and employment retention support to women and gender expansive individuals. Call or go online to get started www.dfsocareercenter.org/news-2
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: East Portland Vocational Rehab Dept.
description: Call for an intake appointment, based on eligibility. Hours: Mon.- Fri. 8 a.m.-5 p.m. Bus: 15, 20. MAX: Blue.
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Easterseals
description: Senior Community Services Employment Program (55+); Homeless Veterans Reintegration Program (HVRP); SSVF homelessness prevention and housing transition for veterans. 237 NE Broadway Ste 100. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8:30 a.m.-5 p.m. Bus: 17, 77. www.easterseals.com/oregon
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member

name: Easterseals Connecting Communities
description: En Español: 503-335-5900 Vocational rehabilitation services for individuals with disabilities who speak English as a second language. ESL and computer classes, job development and job placement; translation and interpretation, resume and interview workshops; ESL support and referral. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8:30 a.m.-5 p.m. Bus: 6

name: Goodwill Job Connection
description: Multiple locations. Free program that develops personalized job search strategy based on individual strengths and goals. Resume assistance, job leads, job fairs and interview practice. Ask to talk to the Employment Specialist. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-5 p.m. meetgoodwill.org/job-connection-home/
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Ground Score Association
description: Peer-led organization creating low-barrier jobs for people experiencing homelessness. Workers paid $20+/hr for litter collection, recycling, and waste management in Portland. GLITTER program (tent-side waste collection), The People's Depot (bottle redemption center), laundry/textile programs. No drug test or SSN required to start. 95% of workers were homeless when hired.
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Job Corps
address: 2701 NW Vaughn St. Ste. 151 Portland, OR 97210
description: Provides hands-on career training for 30 trades for economically disadvantaged youth. GED/high school diploma programs. Free residential training program for youth 16-21 who are low in- come, homeless, have documented disability or are in the foster care system. Call for appt. or apply online at pivot.jobcorps.gov Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-4:30 p.m

name: New Avenues for Youth - PAVE
address: 314 SW 9th Ave. Door C Portland, OR 97205
description: Job training, internships and support for finding jobs and apply- ing to college for youth. Email paveinfo@newavenues.org or call to learn more about PAVE and your eligibility for the program. Orientations Mon. 2-4 p.m. and Fri. 10 a.m.-1 p.m.
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Our Just Future
description: Employment programs for people who are/were unhoused, emphasizing living-wage jobs. Seasonal energy hotline for gas/wood/propane/electric bills: 503-405-7877. 24-hour water discount hotline: 503-548-0217. 10550 NE Halsey St. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-5 p.m. www.ourjustfuture.org
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Outside In - ERC
description: Employment and education support for youth ages 18-24. Ser- vices include GED tutoring and testing for everyone ages 18-24. For youth who have been screened into the Homeless Youth Continuum, additional supports include college admission and financial aid assistance, career coaching and exploration, Job Readiness Training, work placements, internships and more. Drop-In/Intake Hours: Mon. 10 a.m.-1 p.m., Thurs. 10 a.m.-1 p.m. and 2-5 p.m.

name: PIVOT Job Corps
address: 2701 NW Vaughn St. Ste. 151 Portland, OR 97210
description: Non-residential, year round school that provides free high school diploma, GED and training for Medical Administrative Assistant and office administration for women and men ages 16-24. Internship/job placement services, gas stipend or monthly bus pass, daily lunch allowance, bi-weekly attendance stipend, cloth- ing closet on site and driver’s education offered. Open house: Wed. 2 p.m. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-4:30 p.m. Bus: 77

name: Relay Resources
description: A nonprofit organization creating competitive and integrated em- ployment opportunities for people with disabilities. Careers in janitorial, landscape, administrative, product assembly, supply chain and more. Call for help or check out the website at www.relayresources.org

name: Stone Soup
description: Social venture providing training for those facing barriers to employment and housing insecurity. The 12-week job training program provides hands-on culinary skills while feeding resi- dents, businesses and those on the front lines of food insecurity across the city. To join, fill out an application on our website. Call Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-5 p.m. for more info. www.stonesouppdx.com
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Street Roots
description: Immediate work opportunities through vendor program. Ven- dors are able to become a seller of the weekly publication after completing an orientation. Orientations held every Wed. and Fri. at 2:15 p.m. Downtown Bus, MAX. www.streetroots.org
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: The DPI Group
address: 4950 NE MLK Jr. Blvd. Portland, OR 97211
description: Jobs for people with barriers to employment, such as a disabili- ty, chronic medical issue, in recovery or criminal record. Tempo- rary staffing division hires for light industrial and administrative jobs. Security division hires security officers, with DPSST training provided. Northwest Success hires janitorial staff. Clients with- out access to computers can call for appointment. Staffing is THC friendly for non-government jobs. View open positions and apply at the dpigroup.com/jobs. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-5 p.m. Bus: 6, 72.
req: Serves people with criminal records or recently released

name: Voz Workers’ Rights Education Project
description: Empowers immigrant and day laborers by providing economic opportunities, leadership development, education and advocacy to protect their rights and improve their working conditions. New member orientation every other week, please call to sign up. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 7:30 a.m.-2 p.m., Sat. 7:30 a.m.-noon. Bus: 12, 70, 75, 77, MAX: Red, Blue
req: Serves immigrants, refugees, and mixed-status families

name: NE Portland: 30 N Webster St., Ste. E.
description: Gresham: 18633 SE Stark St. Ste. 201 503-669-7112
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: SE Portland: 6401 SE Foster Rd.
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Albina Head Start
description: Early Head Start (childcare and family support for children 6 weeks to 3 years old) and Head Start (preschool and family support for 3 to 5 year olds) provides school readiness, family advocacy and educational, health, nutritional, social and other services. Home-based education program available. Call 503- 236-9389 for Early Head Start, or 503-282-1975 for Head Start. More information at www.albinahs.org
req: Serves families with children and parents/caregivers

name: Baby Blues Connection
description: 24-hour message line. Free telephone, email, online and in-per- son group support and resources to women and their families experiencing any variety of perinatal and postnatal mood disor- ders. www.babybluesconnection.org
req: Serves families with children and parents/caregivers

name: Bethlehem Children’s Clothes Closet
description: Free clothing and other supplies for kids preemie through 17, while supplies last. Expectant parents welcome.The clothing closet is by appointment only. Call or visit smpdx.org to make an appointment.

name: Boys and Girls Aid
description: All-options counseling for unexpected pregnancy; parenting, adoption, or abortion. Call or text 24/7
req: Serves families with children and parents/caregivers

name: Center for Family Success
description: Parenting programs and family stabilization services for jus- tice-involved parents. Parenting skills classes, mentoring program for children and youths. Early childhood home visiting program also available. Call for intake appointment or see www. thepathfindernetwork.org for more information. Hours: Mon.- Thurs. 9 a.m.-7 p.m. and Fri. 9 a.m.-5 p.m
req: Serves families with children and parents/caregivers

name: Assistance for qualifying families to pay for child care services
description: through Community Childcare Initiative (CCI) program. Parents searching for child care, call 211. Phone hours: Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.- 4:30 p.m.
req: Serves families with children and parents/caregivers

name: Jewish Family and Child Service
description: Call to make an appointment. Sliding scale; providing a wide range of counseling group and individual services for individu- als, couples and families. Call to make an appointment.
req: Sliding-scale fees based on income — free for those who cannot pay

name: Mother and Child Education Center
description: Spanish Line: 503-913-9360 (text only) Serves anyone caring for a child. Childbirth, safe sleep, lactation and newborn care workshops and classes; maternity clothes, prenatals and emergency supplies for babies and toddlers. Last appointment is one hour before close. Appointments are pre- ferred. Hours: Mon., Tues., Thurs. 10 a.m.-5 p.m., Wed. 1-5 p.m. and Fri. 10 a.m.-3 p.m. Bus: 12, 66, 77, 75, MAX. www.momchildpdx.org
req: Pregnant, postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 (WIC)

name: Mult. Co. Family Resolution Services
address: 1200 SW First Ave. Ste. 3400 Portland OR, 97204
description: Provides services to help parents navigate personal decisions, meet legal requirements and make healthy plans for children re- lated to separation and divorce, including Mediation and Parent Education. For more information, check out the website at www. multco.us/family-resolution. Call or email: family.resolution@ multco.us for assistance. Walk-in hours: Mon-Fri. 8:30 a.m-4 p.m. Downtown Bus, MAX.
req: Serves families with children and parents/caregivers

name: Multnomah Early Childhood Program
description: Early intervention and early childhood special education services for children, birth to age 5. Services are individually designed to address the special needs of young children with developmental delays or disabilities. Free of charge

name: Northwest Family Services
description: Counseling, workshops and classes. Outpatient, sliding scale counseling for adult individuals and couples, available in English and Spanish, in-person and virtual. Call for appt. Other self-re- ferred services include parenting classes, MHFA trainings and family programs. More info and registration is online at nwfs.org.
req: Sliding-scale fees based on income — free for those who cannot pay

name: SEI Community & Family Programs
description: Multiple programs: parent support for families with children prenatal to age 6 (home visits, activity kits, developmental screenings); culturally specific DV/SA crisis intervention and case management; energy assistance for PGE/Pacific Power/NW Natural. Call for services: DV/SA 503-972-3698; Family 503-285-0493; Energy 503-713-5590.
req: Pregnant, postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 (WIC)

name: All Saints Episcopal Church - Woodstock Food Pantry
description: 503-777-3829 Groceries provided via drive-through or walk-up pantry. Hours: Fri. and Sat. noon-2 p.m. Bus: 19, 75. www.allsaintspdx.org

name: Allen Temple Emergency Aid Center
description: Food boxes. Service area from NE Broadway to N Lombard and NE 42nd to N Greeley. Hours: 11 a.m.-2 p.m. Wed., Sat. Closed 1st Wed. and Sat. of the month. Bus: 6. Black Community Food Center 4450 NE 131st Pl. Portland, OR. 97230 Food pantry open to anyone that has a food need. Available to shop once a week. Please bring 2 bags and folks can shop once a week. Hours: Wed. 2-4 p.m. Thurs., 3-6 p.m., Fri. 2-4 p.m., 1-2 p.m. for Seniors 55 and up. feedemfreedom.org

name: Hand Up Project
address: 4115 N. Mississippi Ave. Ste. 5 Portland, OR 97217
description: Provides a shopping-style food pantry service in which people can choose the food that works best for them. Located at the Q Center Mon. and Tues. noon-4 p.m. and 4th Sat. of the month from 10 a.m.-1 p.m. www.handupproject.org

name: Lift Urban Portland
description: Shopping-style food pantry for residents in Downtown & North- west Portland located at First United Methodist Church. Shop- pers welcome twice per month. Monthly Delivered Food Boxes available to residents of select buildings, with referral. Thurs. and Fri. 3-6 p.m. Tues. by appointment only. Bus line: 24, 72. Love Our City Community Food Pantry 6120 NE 57th Ave. Portland, OR 97218 Pre-packed food boxes offered at no cost. Boxes typically contain dry goods, bread, fresh produce, dairy, eggs, meat and ready to eat meals (as available.) Hours: Tues. 11 a.m.-noon

name: Mainspring
description: Free clothing once per month; personal care products when available. Tues./Thurs. 10 a.m.-1 p.m. Small overnight food bags upon request Mon.-Thurs. 9 a.m.-3 p.m.; onsite food pantry 3rd Wed. 10 a.m.-2 p.m.; four mobile food pantry locations monthly. Bus: 24, 72. www.mainspringpdx.org
req: Limited visits per time period — call for current policy

name: Metro Church of Christ
description: Emergency food boxes. Use west entrance. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 11 a.m.-1 p.m. Bus: 4. MAX Blue.

name: Multnomah County SNAP Outreach Program
description: Mobile team providing on-site SNAP application assistance in Mult. Co. area to individuals and families. Schedule appts. by phone (call or text). Hours: 8 a.m.-5 p.m. Evenings and week- ends by appt

name: Neighborhood House
description: Limited deliveries in the nearby SW area. To order a food box to be delivered, please use the online order form. Food boxes two times per month. Portland city limits west of the Willamette and Lake Oswego. Hours: 1 a.m.-noon and 1-4 p.m. Mon. and Wed.; 2-6 p.m. Thurs. Bus: 44, 45. www.nhpdx.org

name: Northeast Emergency Food Program
description: Free groceries are available indoors, self-serve style. Clients may shop one time each week. Please bring your own bags. No geographic boundaries. Hours: Tuesday 4-6:30 p.m., Thursday and Saturday 9:30 a.m.-noon. Call or check the website for cur- rent information. NEFP does not provide food delivery, clothing or rental assistance. Bus: 71, 75. www.emo-nefp.org

name: Oregon Food Bank
description: Multiple locations. Referral service to help find perishable and non-perishable food assistance. Resources can include fresh produce, protein options, canned and dry goods; varies by location. Visit Food Finder for locations, days and times, available in 19 languages. oregonfoodfinder.org Phone hours: Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-5 p.m.

name: SnowCap Community Charities
description: Pre-packed food boxes with fresh fruits and vegetables when available. Hours: Mon.-Fri.10 a.m.-2 p.m. Mon. & Wed. 6-8 p.m.

name: St. Mark’s Lutheran Church
description: No-charge food pantry every Fri. Hours: 2-5 p.m. Bring a bag if possible. Serving all zip codes, and you can come every week.. Enter the parking lot on 54th Ave. and proceed to double doors.

name: St. Vincent de Paul
description: Food boxes every 3rd Fri. 1-3:30 p.m. (Clackamas & Multnomah counties). One-time rent help to avoid eviction when funds available — call Mon.-Thurs. 9:30 a.m.-noon & 1-3:30 p.m. 8101 SE Cornwell St. Bus: 72, MAX Green. 503-235-8431

name: Oregon DHS – SNAP Benefits Offices (Multnomah County)
description: Apply for SNAP (Oregon Trail Card / food stamps), Medicaid, and other benefits at Multnomah County DHS Self-Sufficiency offices. Locations include: Five Oak Downtown (290 SW 5th Ave), Alberta (30 N Webster St), Northeast, Southeast, and others. Also apply online at one.oregon.gov.

name: The Salvation Army
description: Client choice food pantries. Gresham Family Services Hours: Mon.-Thur. 9 a.m.-noon Moore Street Family Services 5335 N. Williams Ave., Portland, OR 97217 Hours: Wed. & Fri. 9 a.m.-1 p.m., Family night meal and Bible Study Tues. 5-7 p.m.

name: William Temple House
description: Food pick-up twice monthly via drop-in shopping/lottery system. Includes health and hygiene items. Tues.-Thurs. 11 a.m.-2 p.m. Individual/couples/family counseling on sliding scale, by appointment Mon.-Thurs. 9 a.m.-8 p.m. 2023 NW Hoyt St. Bus: 15, 20, 77, Streetcar. www.williamtemple.org
req: Sliding-scale fees based on income — free for those who cannot pay

name: Women, Infants, and Children (WIC)
description: Provides monthly healthy food benefits, growth monitoring and referral to other programs for pregnant and post-partum women, infants and children up to age 5. Call for appointment or text APPT TODAY to 20121
req: Pregnant, postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 (WIC)

name: Zarephath Pantry
address: 59 NW Ava Ave. Gresham, OR 97030 (enter property through
description: parking lot in back) Food boxes plus many items to choose from. Bring your own empty bags if possible. Clients with housing served twice per month. Unhoused clients served every week. Closed major holidays. Winter weather closures follow Gresham-Barlow School District. Hours: Thurs. 10 a.m.-1 p.m. (Be in line by 12:30.) Met- ro bus 9 at Eastman Parkway

name: Urban Gleaners
description: Free food markets at 20+ locations throughout the Portland metro area.

name: Feed'em Freedom – Black Community Food Center
description: Food pantry, sustainable agriculture, and food justice education for Black community.

name: PDX Free Fridge Network
description: Free food in community fridges and pantries across Portland. Take what you need, leave what you can.

name: Milk Crate Kitchen
description: Cooked family meals twice a month via home delivery and in-person pickup.
hours: Twice monthly – see website to request a meal

name: Equitable Giving Circle – Weekly Free Market
description: Weekly free market for Black and Brown folks, plus health and wellness community events.

name: Sunshine Division
description: Free food box delivery and pantry by appointment for Portland residents.

name: NE Emergency Food Program
description: Emergency food program serving Northeast Portland.

name: Portland Adventist Community Services Food Pantry
description: Food pantry services.

name: William Temple House Food Pantry
description: Food pantry and social services.

name: Oregon Food Bank Food Finder

Walk- in inpatient admission: Mon.-Fri. 6:45-7:45 a.m. Outpatient: Mon.-Fri. 1-4 p.m.

name: Imani Center
description: Mental health care, outpatient addiction treatment, counseling, peer support and employment and housing support for the Black and African American community. For more information, please visit centralcityconcern.org/recoverylocation/imani-cen- ter/

name: Letty Owings Center
description: Intensive residential drug and alcohol treatment for single women who are pregnant or with children 5 years and younger. Women with no insurance can receive assistance in enrolling.
req: Pregnant, postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 (WIC)

name: Call
description: at centralcityconcern.org/recovery-location/letty-owings-center

name: Puentes
description: Linguistically and culturally appropriate outpatient mental health treatment for Latino adults, and substance use disorder treatment for adults, including DUII treatment.

name: Crystal Meth Anonymous of Oregon
description: (24/7 help line) Free support for individuals looking to recover from addiction to crystal meth. Twelve-step fellowship offered. Meeting times, locations and additional info can be found at crystalmeth.org

name: David Romprey Oregon Warmline 1-800-698-2392
description: 24/7 support line staffed by a caring team of trained peers offering nonjudgmental and confidential peer support every day. Also offer support in Spanish.
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: Dougy Center
description: Free peer grief support groups for children (3-18) and adults (18-40) and their families, who have experienced the death of a parent, sibling or significant person in their lives. Pathways Program (SE Portland only): advanced or terminal illness. Kids, teens, adult care, group and adults with illness. Group sites in Portland, Beaverton and Canby. Bilingual Spanish groups avail- able. Portland hours: Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-5 p.m. www.dougy.org

name: Dual Diagnosis Anonymous of Oregon
description: Peer support program based on a version of 12-step AA with 5 additional steps focused on mental wellness. Numerous meet- ing times including evenings and weekends. Directory online. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 9:30 a.m.-1:30 p.m. ddainc.org

name: El Jardín
address: 10209 SE Division St. Building B Ste. 100 Portland, OR 97266
description: Bilingual (Spanish and English) services to any self-identifying Latino seeking support with a substance-use disorder. Services include free harm reduction resources, peer support services and case management/systems navigation support. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 11 a.m.-7 p.m..

name: Fora Treatment Center
address: 10230 SE Cherry Blossom Dr. Portland, OR 97216
description: Programs include detox, medication assisted treatment (MAT), medically monitored treatment, residential, outpatient, state-certified DUII services; family therapy and peer mentor- ing. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-5 p.m. Detox: Mon.-Sat. starting at 7:30 a.m. Mon.-Sun.: OHP patients only. Downtown Bus, MAX. forahealth.org

name: Lewis & Clark Problem Gambling Services
address: 4445 SW Barbur Blvd. Ste. 205 (upstairs) Portland, OR 97239
description: Free counseling for individuals, families and friends who are impacted by gambling. Email pgsappointments@lclark.edu to make an appointment. Hours: Mon, Tues. & Fri. 9 a.m.-7 p.m.; Sat. & Sun. 9 a.m.-4 p.m.

name: LifeWorks Northwest
description: Multiple Locations. Integrated approach combining behavioral health care, primary health care, outpatient mental health and addiction treatment for adults and youth. Hours and services vary by location, please call for intake or see the website at www.lifeworksnw.org for more information.

name: Lines for Life Alcohol and Drug Helpline
description: For individuals and family members seeking crisis intervention, treatment referral and chemical dependency information. Calls are answered 24/7. Text “RecoveryNow” to 839863. www. linesforlife.org

name: Miracles Club
address: 4200 NE MLK Jr. Blvd. Portland, OR 97212
description: Culturally-specific recovery services for African Americans, but no one is turned away. Narcotics Anonymous meetings, employ- ment and life skills help, acupuncture, monthly expungement clinics, transportation support and certified recovery mentor training quarterly. Peer services and phone hours: Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-5 p.m. Bus: 6. Apply for services online at www.miracles- club.org/apply

name: NARA NW Outpatient Treatment Services
description: Outpatient treatment for chemical dependency. Family-oriented recovery. Call for admission coordinator. Intakes performed Mon.-Fri. Hours: 8:30 a.m.-5 p.m www.naranorthwest.org

name: New Narrative Mill Park Center
description: Formerly known as Luke-Dorf Eastside Clinic. Outpatient mental health treatment including counseling, case management and medication management. Not a walk-in clinic; call for intake. Clinical Services to support client wellness. Hours: Wed. 9:30 a.m.-4:30 p.m. www.newnarrativepdx.org

name: Northwest Treatment
description: Outpatient mental health services including counseling and DUI and court mandated treatment. Spanish available. Hours: Mon.- Fri. 8:30 a.m.-noon, 1-5 p.m

name: Oregon Change Clinic
description: Provides short-term housing and culturally specific stabilization services for adults in early recovery. Trauma-informed care, peer support, case management, and connections to ongoing treat- ment. Specialize in serving Black, Indigenous, and People of Col- or (BIPOC) while remaining inclusive to all individuals impacted by substance use, houselessness, and systemic barriers. More info at www.oregonchangeclinic.com. Hours: Mon.-Thurs. 9 a.m.- 5 p.m. Fri. 9 a.m.-3:30 p.m. Downtown Bus, MAX

name: Painted Horse Recovery
address: 10209 SE Division St. Bldg. D Portland, OR 97266
description: Drop-in recovery center for Native Americans and all who are looking for culturally specific services. Services include 12-step meetings, peer services delivered by Certified Recovery Men- tors, recovery events, skill building groups and culture nights for Native American families. Hours: 2-10 p.m. daily

name: Portland Rescue Mission - Shepherd’s Door
description: 5-week residential Discovery Program for mothers with children. Parenting support, onsite daycare and programming for children age 10 and younger provided. Upon completion, participants may either graduate or enter the New Life Program, a 12-month Christian-based program. Addiction recovery, life skills develop- ment, legal and healthcare advocacy and job-readiness. Bus: 77 Call to apply. www.portlandrescuemission.org

name: Portland Rescue Mission - The Harbor
description: 5-week residential Discovery Program for men providing sta- bilization, community and recovery support. Upon completion, participants may either graduate or enter the free New Life Program, a 12-month Christian-based program. Addiction recov- ery, life skills development, legal and healthcare advocacy and job-readiness. Call the intake line or apply in-person at Portland Rescue Mission’s Burnside Shelter (111 W. Burnside St.) Mon.- Fri. at 11 a.m. Bus: #12, Max: Red

name: Project Respond
description: Mobile crisis team for individuals of any age in the county ex- periencing a mental health crisis, 24/7 service. Provides safety assessments and planning as well as resource connection.

name: Providence Addiction Treatment Services
address: 5228 NE Hoyt St. Bldg. B Portland, OR 97213
description: Adult outpatient services, DUII and other related programs. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-5:30 p.m. Bus: 20. www.providence.org/ oregon

name: Recovery Café Roseway
description: Community supporting each other in recovery from addiction, trauma, abuse, incarceration, houselessness, mental illness and isolation. Meals, support groups, classes and peer support services are free and open to everyone. Membership is based on three requirements: do not be under the influence of drugs or alcohol while at the café, participate in a weekly support circle and give back to the community in some way. Hours: Tues.-Thurs. 11 a.m.-4 p.m.

name: Recovery Works NW
description: Mental health and substance use rteatment program offering fast access to medications (MAT/MSR) services (Sublocade, Su- boxone, Subutex, Vivitrol, Naltrexone, support medications), and outpatient treatment, peer support services, and DUII services. OHP and most private insurance plans accepted. Hours: Mon. and Wed. 9 a.m.-4 a.m. Tues. and Thurs. 9 a.m.-6 p.m. Fri. 9 a.m.-3 a.m. Call for more info

name: The Royal Rose Foundation
description: Free care packages, resource navigation, harm-reduction sup- plies and trainings, youth leadership activities, and wellness-fo- cused community events for people impacted by systemic barriers. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 10 a.m.–4 p.m. (call ahead to confirm availability)

name: True Colors Recovery
address: 3807 NE Martin Luther King Jr Blvd. Portland, OR 97212
description: Substance Use Recovery Support. LGBTQIA+ specific, but not exclusive, drop-in center with a wide range of substance use recovery meetings every day. Variety of social and health events every month. Free Peer Mentor services. Hours: Sun.-Fri. 2-10 p.m

name: Union Gospel Mission - LifeChange Recovery
description: Drug and alcohol residential program for men. Offers Chris- tian-based recovery community where residents receive individ- ual counseling, spiritual and life skills classes and work therapy. Call or go to website for appointment & interview. Downtown Bus, MAX. www.ugmportland.org

name: Union Gospel Mission - Angela’s House
description: Drug and alcohol residential program for women and children. Offers Christian-based recovery community where residents receive individual counseling, spiritual and life skills classes, job readiness, education and a children’s activity center. Call or go to website for appointment & interview.

name: URS Club
description: Recovery meetings: AA, NA, GA, Al-Anon and Criminon. Call, drop in or go to ursclub.com for meeting schedules. Bus: 20

name: White Grape Community Services
description: Provide compassionate and accessible mental health services for individuals and families. Problem gambling support, therapy, peer support, crisis intervention and personalized treatment planning. Clothing services and SIM card applications (Wed. 1-4 p.m.) also offered. Call or drop-in Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-5 p.m

name: Animal Aid
description: Animal rescue and shelter offering referrals to community resources, including veterinary financial assistance, low-cost spay/neuter options and pet food banks. Resource list also available on website. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 11 a.m.-4 p.m. Sat. noon- 4 p.m. Bus: 54, 56. www.animalaidpdx.org
req: Serves pet owners experiencing poverty or homelessness
Income or geographic eligibility may apply

name: Fern’s Place
description: Provide a safe, temporary care environment for the pets of indi- viduals seeking in-patient medical care or addiction treatment. To place a pet fill out the form at sites.google.com/view/fernsplaceinc/place-a-pet or call
req: Serves pet owners experiencing poverty or homelessness
Income or geographic eligibility may apply

name: PAW Team
description: Basic veterinary care including examinations, vaccinations, deworming, flea treatment and limited spay/neuter surger- ies available by appointment only. Clients must be below the federal poverty line, and documentation may be required for veterinary care. Free pet supply bank open to the public. Hours: Tues.-Thurs. 10 a.m.-3 p.m. Bus: 72, 77; MAX Blue, Green, Red.

name: Spay and Save 1-800-345-7729
description: Low-cost spay and neuter services for cats of low-income peo- ple. Vaccinations offered at time of spay/neuter. Qualified pet owners must be receiving OHP, Apple Health, Medicaid, WIC, SNAP, SSI, Section 8, TANF, public housing, free or reduced school lunches. Must be a resident of Mult., Wash., Clack. or Clark Cos. Options for scheduling online at www.asapmetro.org/ spay-save/

name: The Pongo Fund
description: Provides high-quality dog and/or cat food, possible veterinary care referral and more per service guidelines. First-time visitors, please call at least 7 days in advance or register by emailing info@thepongofund.org
req: Serves pet owners experiencing poverty or homelessness
Income or geographic eligibility may apply

name: Flip the Script
description: Reentry program that offers culturally specific services, includ- ing navigation, direct support services, resources, and encour- agement to African American and Black adults being released from prison.
req: Serves people with criminal records or recently released

name: Fresh Out Community-Based Reentry
address: 4200 NE Martin Luther King Jr Blvd. Portland, OR 97211
description: or 503-819-1695 Help underserved and overrepresented Black and African Amer- icans transition from prison back into society. Assist the for- merly incarcerated with bus fare, food, clothing and mentoring in hopes of ending the cycle of recidivism. Offer employment, housing, and behavioral health referrals, peer-to-peer counsel- ing and family support services. Call or email freshoutpdx@gmail.com
req: Serves people with criminal records or recently released

name: Helping Hands Reentry Outreach Centers
address: 14355 N Bybee Lake Ct. Portland, OR 97203
description: Trauma-informed, data-driven, person-centered homelessness and reentry services. Locations in Clatsop, Lincoln, Multnomah, Tillamook and Yamhill Counties in Oregon. The reentry program offers employment and recovery support and life skills classes. Require drug and alcohol screenings and mandatory community service hours. Intake hours: 8 a.m.- 5 p.m. daily
req: Serves people with criminal records or recently released

name: Mult. Co. Assessment & Referral Center
address: 421 SW Fifth Ave. Ste. 600 Portland, OR 97204
description: Transitional services for inmates returning to the community. Services to stabilize offenders who are engaged in community supervision. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-5 p.m.
req: Serves people with criminal records or recently released from incarceration

name: Village Resources
description: Recovery and support services for adults after release from Or- egon correctional facilities. Supplies each individual with basic daily living resources that are essential in navigating the first to second month after re-entry
req: Serves people with criminal records or recently released from incarceration

name: 211info 211
description: Call for recording of up-to-date info on utility and rental assis- tance in Mult. Co. Text your zip code to 898211 or check web- site for updates. www.211info.org
req: Documentation of housing need required (lease, eviction notice, or utility shutoff)

name: Fair Housing Council of Oregon
description: Assist in issues of illegal housing discrimination. Specifically evaluate violations of the federal Fair Housing Act and asso- ciated state & local laws that protect against discriminatory acts and different treatment. Fair Housing laws protect against different treatment in housing based on an individual’s protect- ed status including race, national origin, religion, sex, familial status, disability, marital status, domestic violence survivorship, sexual orientation, gender expression, and source of income. If you feel you have been discriminated against, call or email enforcement@fhco.org

name: Resolutions Northwest
address: 4531 SE Belmont St. Ste. 300 Portland, OR 97215
description: Mediation service geared towards assisting renters and housing providers to resolve conflicts and prevent eviction. Services cov- er issues ranging from repairs, rent disputes, property damage, noise complaints and more. Assist with improving tenant-land- lord relations, maintaining current housing for those most im- pacted, and mediating neighbor-to-neighbor, community issues, schools, and workplace conflicts. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-5 p.m
req: Documentation of housing need required (lease, eviction notice, or utility shutoff)

name: Rental and utility assitance when funds are available for those
description: living between I-5 and NE 28th and between Shaver and Ainsworth. Located in the Martha H. Terrell Community Center. Hours: Wed.-Fri. 10 a.m-noon. Bus: 72.
req: Documentation of housing need required (lease, eviction notice, or utility shutoff)

name: Multnomah County Emergency Weather Shelters 211
description: Check webpage multco.us/cold or call 211 to stay up to date on warming shelters and other Multnomah County resources to stay safe in severe weather during the winter months. Agape Village Low-barrier emergency overnight congregate shelters for adult men and women.
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Portland Central Church of the Nazarene
description: Hours: 9 p.m.-6:30 a.m. daily. No line-up before 8:30 p.m. Phone only answered 8 p.m.- 11 p.m.
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: St. Stephens Episcopal Church
description: Hours: 9 p.m.-7 a.m. daily. No line-up before 8:30 p.m. Phone only answered 8 p.m.- 11 p.m
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Bybee Lakes Hope Center
address: 14355 N Bybee Lake Ct. Portland, OR 97203
description: Serves homeless men, women and families with children. Participants must be ready to be clean and sober, and must not be registered sex offenders of any class. Referrals to the Hope Center are accepted from registered community partners, call for help in locating a community partner.

name: CityTeam Portland
description: 6-month residential faith-based recovery program for men (screening Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-noon, 209 NW 4th Ave). Emergency overnight shelter for men (vouchers 5:45 p.m., $5 cash 6:30 p.m., free after 8 p.m., check-in daily 6:30 p.m.-6 a.m., 526 SE Grand Ave). MAT clinic for substance use disorders at 1030 NE Couch St — call for hours. 503-231-9334
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Voluntary Isolation & Medical Respite Shelter
description: The Northeast 82nd Ave Voluntary Isolation Motel, operated by Do Good Multnomah, offers medical isolation rooms for up to two weeks for those experiencing contagious illness or seeking respite care. Breakfast and dinner are served daily. Couples are welcome, as well as one pet per person.
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Shelters
description: Spectrum of shelter opportunities for those experiencing house- lessness in the Portland Metro area. Including low-barrier con- gregate, clean & sober, motels, villages, and housing retention support. All shelters have a Veteran-preference. Shelters provide meal services, showers and hygiene care, support services, case management, and housing navigation. Individuals age 18 and older
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member

name: Family Promise of Metro East
description: Serves families with children (any combination of adults with one or more minor children). Does not accept clients fleeing domestic violence. Phone hours: Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-5 p.m.

name: Janus Youth Programs - Porch Light Shelter
description: Shelter for youth ages 16-24 experiencing homelessness. Open every night of the year from 8:45 pm until 8:45 am. Youth may self-refer at the door any time in the night between 9 p.m. and 5 a.m. Beds are first come, first served. Bus: 15, 20, 51. Max: Red, Blue.
req: Must be experiencing homelessness or unstable housing

name: My Father’s House
description: Address confidential. (Gresham) Six-month shelter program for families with children. Case man- agement, life skills, curriculum, Rent Well classes and employ- ment requirements. Call for information
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: New Avenues for Youth - Alba Collaborative
description: Emergency shelter for youth ages 9-17, case management, mentorship and family mediation. Open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.

name: Our Just Future – Family Emergency Shelters
description: Multiple 24-hour emergency shelters for families and marginalized genders in SE Portland and Gresham. Lilac Meadows (SE Portland) serves families with at least one dependent child. Call 211 for intake. Also operates energy and utility assistance hotlines and seasonal energy programs.

name: Path Home-Family Village 211
description: 24-hour, trauma-informed shelter for 17 homeless families with children. Amenities include individual private rooms, meals, showers, laundry, communal spaces including a computer lab and library, rapid-rehousing support and culturally-specific pro- gramming. Available to families who are currently experiencing homelessness with children under 18 in their care. Call 211 or text your zip code to 898211 to be placed on Multnomah Coun- ty’s waitlist. www.path-home.org Portland City Shelter Services - Alternative Shelter Sites 311 Multiple Locations. Village-style tiny homes and limited RV parking with shared restrooms, showers, laundry and at least one meal a day provid- ed. Include case management with wraparound behavioral and mental health services. Menlo, Reedway, Multnomah, Clinton
req: Must be experiencing homelessness or unstable housing
Waitlist may apply — call for current wait time

name: Triangle, N. Portland (70 RV spots), SW Naito (for LGBTQIA2S+
description: individuals), NE Weidler (priority for BIPOC individuals) can only be accessed by referral. For self-referral, call 311 and request an outreach worker
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Portland Rescue Mission
description: Free emergency shelter for men nightly. 24/7 drop-in: restrooms, hygiene, socks, water, mail. Free clothing for men Tues. 1-2 p.m. Dining room: breakfast 7-8 a.m. & dinner 6-7 p.m. daily. Call 503-906-7690. 111 W Burnside St, Portland.
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Moore Street Shelter (men and women)
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Northrup Shelter (men and women)
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: SAFES Shelter (women only)
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Transition Projects
description: Offers short-term residential and emergency shelter programs for individuals of all gender identities and couples. Shelter hours, services and locations vary. For space in one of the shel- ters, visit the Resource Center or fill out the following form: www. tprojects.org/shelter-access Resource Center Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8:30 a.m.-7 p.m. Sat.-Sun. 8:30 a.m.-3:30 p.m. Bus: 17, 33; MAX Green, Yellow. WeShine Multiple Locations Village-style alternative shelter sites that prioritize folks who are Black, Indigenous or People of Color. Three locations: Avalon (SE Portland) for femme-aligned individuals, Parkrose (NE Port- land) for LGBTQIA+ individuals and St. Andrews (N Portland) for individuals 45+ and/or with physical disabilities. Apply online at

name: Multnomah County STI Clinic
description: Affordable, confidential, non-judgemental STI testing. Accepts OHP, Medicare and Medicaid. No one turned away for inability to pay. Call for appointment. Phone hours: Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-5 p.m. Clinical Services: Mon.-Thurs. 9 a.m.-4:30 p.m
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: OHSU Partnership Project
address: 2525 SW 1st Ave. Ste. 1201 Bldg. B-West Portland, OR 97201
description: Comprehensive array of services for people living with HIV, including medical case management, as well as service naviga- tion for immigrants and refugees living with HIV. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-5 p.m. Bus: 9, 12, 35, 40, 44 MAX Orange. Free parking under the building (take ticket to be validated). Cabs and ride services can drop you off at pull-in spots off of Arthur Ave
req: Serves immigrants, refugees, and mixed-status families

name: Peer and community support for women living with HIV/AIDS.
description: Hours: Mon., Wed., Fri. 8 a.m.-5 p.m. Outpatient Program infor- mation and meal on Nutrition Night Thurs. 6 p.m. in Quest build- ing or call intake coordinator. Bus: 19, 20. www.quest-center.org

name: Everly Project
description: Outreach services monthly, distribute harm reduction kits in- cluding safer injection and safer smoking kits, naloxone, wound care kits, extreme weather supplies and clothing. Second Fri. of every month at Ground Score resource fair 624 NW Couch St in Old Town. Email hello@everlyproject.org for more information.

name: Multnomah County Harm Reduction Clinic
address: 12425 NE Glisan St. Portland, OR 97230 (in Menlo Park Plaza,
description: behind Walgreens) Syringe exchange, sexual health services, overdose rescue kits, and fentanyl test strips. Hours: Mon. and Thurs. 11 a.m.-2 p.m and 3 p.m.-7 p.m.

name: Outside In - Drug Users Health Services
description: Health and harm reduction services for adults ages 18 and older. Syringe exchange, safer smoking supplies, drug supply checking, safer sex supplies, HIV, Hep C and Syphilis testing. Hours: Mon.-Fri. noon-5 p.m

name: Portland People’s Outreach Project (PPOP)
description: Friday Evening Outreach: 6-7 p.m. Eastbank Esplanade at SE Main St. 7:25-7:50 p.m. Greyhound/Bud Clark NW 6th & Hoyt 8-8:45 p.m. West side, please text/call for location Saturday Afternoon Outreach: 1-4 p.m. St. Johns - The Cut (Peninsula Crossing Trail), on the bike path just west of N. Lombard & Macrum Sunday Afternoon Outreach: 2:30-3:15 p.m. Hollywood Transit Center – end of NE Weidler off 42nd 3:45 p.m.-4:30 p.m. South Park Blocks - SW Park & Jefferson 4:45 p.m.-5:30 p.m. Greyhound/Bud Clark NW 6th & Hoyt

name: Ainsworth. Call or drop in at Martha H. Terrell Community Cen-
description: ter. Hours: Wed.-Fri. 10 a.m-noon. Bus: 72

name: Community Resource and Referral Center
address: 308 SW First Ave. Portland, OR 97204 (Lawrence Bldg.)
description: Part of the VA Portland Heath Care System. Services for veter- ans who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless. Walk- in hours are Mon., Tues., Wed., Fri. 8 a.m.-3 p.m. and Thurs. 10 a.m.-3 p.m. Downtown Bus; MAX Red, Blue. www.portland. va.gov/locations/crrc. asp
req: Must be a veteran, active duty service member, or eligible family member
DD-214 or military documentation may be required

name: Do Good Multnomah
description: Veteran-priority and Veteran-specific shelter and service provid- er. Options include Veteran-priority bridge housing, permanent housing, low-barrier and alternative shelters. Shelter locations also provide meal services, showers and hygiene care, support services, case management and housing navigation. Call or fill out referral online www.dogoodmultnomah.org/refer
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member

name: Lines for Life Military Helpline 1-888-457-4838
description: Offers help and hope to all veterans, military service members, family members and friends, and providers or caregivers who serve military communities. Operated independently and not affiliated with the Department of Defense, offers connection to resources. Hours: 24/7.
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member

name: Portland Vet Center
description: Readjustment counseling services for combat veterans and their families, also for veterans who experienced military sexual trauma. Grief counseling for families who have lost someone in active service. Other services through VA, state and community agencies also available. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-4:30 p.m. Bus: 73, 77.
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member

name: Returning Veterans Project
description: Free mental and physical health services for Veterans, Current Service, National Guard Members and their Families from Volun- teer Health Providers. Go online to returningveterans.org to see the full provider directory, or call for more information
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member

name: Transition Projects - Mt. Scott Office
address: 7916 SE Foster Rd. Ste. 104 Portland, OR 97206
description: Resources, referrals to VA and homeless prevention assistance, and supportive services for Veteran Families (SSVF) and Grant and Per Diem (GPD) shelter program available. Call for screen- ing and eligibility info or go to www.tprojects.org/veterans-ser- vices and fill out referral form. Veterans Orientation (screening for multiple Vet Programs and shelter referrals): Tues. 9-10 a.m. at the Katherine Gray building (1115 SW 11th Ave. Portland.)
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member

name: Veterans Crisis Line 1-800-273-8255
description: Connects veterans in crisis, their families and friends with VA responders. Press 1 for veterans or text 838255 or chat online. Hours: 24/7
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member

name: Coordinated Access for Runaway and Homeless Youth
description: Youth ages 9 to 17 in crisis and/or in need of a safe place to stay can call or drop in at the Alba Collaborative (located at the New Avenues for Youth Opportunity Center) 24 hours a day 7 days a week. 971-754-4350 Youth ages 16 to 24 experiencing homelessness can call the Access Center for a screening into the Homeless Youth Contin- uum from 9 a.m.-5 p.m. or show up at Porch Light Shelter after 8:45 p.m. for a shelter bed. 503-432-3986 1635 SW Alder St. Portland, Oregon 97205
req: Must be experiencing homelessness or unstable housing

name: AMP (Artist Mentorship Program)
description: Music and art mentorship, meals and community for youth experiencing homelessness ages 15-25. Hours: Mon.-Thurs. 4-8 p.m. plus Fri. by appointment. Bus: 19, 20; MAX Yellow, Green.
req: Must be experiencing homelessness or unstable housing

name: Community Transitional School
description: Free private, nonprofit Pre-K to eighth grade school for children whose families are homeless, in housing transition or children at risk of school failure. Call for information. Enroll over the phone. Bus: 72
req: Call to confirm eligibility and current availability

name: Insights Young Parents Services
description: Support for teen parents and their partners who are younger than 24, including case management, information and recre- ation. Call for screening/referral. Resource closet of children’s items and diapers available by appt. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-5 p.m. Bus: 19. www.insightstpp.org”
req: Call to confirm eligibility and current availability

name: Janus Youth Programs
description: Access Center offers screenings 365 days a year for 16-24 year olds experiencing homelessness. Call 8 a.m.-6 p.m. to make an appointment with a screener. Youth can also show up at Porch Light (open every night of the year 8:45 p.m.-8:45 a.m.) for a bed, or go the day programs at New Avenues for Youth or Out- side In for help connecting to a screener.
req: Must be experiencing homelessness or unstable housing

name: Janus Youth Programs - Yellow Brick Road
description: or 503-708-0617 Outreach program operating every evening 6-9 p.m. to assist homeless young people with immediate safety needs and pro- vide access to shelter, including during severe weather events.
req: Call to confirm eligibility and current availability

name: Lines for Life YouthLine 1-877-968-8491
description: Peer-to-peer crisis and help line for youth ages 10-24. Support for youth in crisis. Staffed by teens 4-10 p.m., calls answered by adults outside these hours. Text teen2teen to 839863. Emails to teen2teen@linesforlife.org. Chat online at theyouthline.org/

name: NARA NW Youth Center
description: Child and family services include mental health assessments, services and counseling. Youth residential and outpatient treat- ent offering holistic and culturally specific advocacy services. Call for intake information. Youth Programming also includes groups, outings, life skills training, suicide prevention. Call 971- 279-4838 for information on general youth programming. Native American Youth and Family Center (NAYA) 503-288-8177 5135 NE Columbia Blvd. Portland, OR 97218 No walk-ins, call for appt. Bridge to Construction (ages 18- 26) is a 9-week pre-apprenticeship for high school graduates preparing for a career in the trades. Both programs include stipends, individualized coaching and help launching into liv- ing-wage careers. Year-round enrollment opportunities. Call or email apply@pybpdx.org to apply!

Child advocacy center at 1713 Penn Lane, Oregon City: forensic interviews, medical assessments, therapy for abuse/neglect (call Mon.-Fri. 8:30 a.m.-5 p.m.). 39140 Proctor Blvd, Sandy. 503-668-7962. www.antfarmyouthservices.com

name: School Homeless Liaisons
description: Any student who is unsheltered, couch surfing or living in an insecure housing situation is eligible for support through The McKinney-Vento Act’s Education of Houseless Children and Youth Program including transportation to school district of origin when feasible. Call or email housing@nclack.k12.or.us to be connected with services
req: Call to confirm eligibility and current availability

name: Youth Era - Clackamas Drop
description: Drop-in space for youth ages 14-21. Snacks, computer access, music lab, pool table, clothing closet, job support and opportuni- ties to meet new people and build community. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 3-6 p.m

name: FolkTime – Oregon City Peer Connection Center
description: Free drop-in peer support for adults 18+ with lived experience of mental health or substance use. Part of FolkTime's network of peer-run programs serving Clackamas County. Intentional Peer Support groups, community activities, one-on-one peer support at Clackamas County Health Centers. Also provides peer support at Sandy Behavioral Health Center — no need to live in Sandy to access.

name: Clothes for Kids
description: Free clothing for low-income students attending Beaverton schools. Must make an appointment over the phone or through a ParentSquare account. Clothing not provided to adults, infants or toddlers. Bus: 67, MAX: Blue.

name: Elevate Him
address: 1513 SW Marlow St. Ste. A Portland, OR 97225
description: Men’s resource center offering clothes, supportive men’s groups, haircuts and styling. Call to make an appointment
req: Some programs limit visits per month

name: Helping Hands Clothes Closet
description: Adult and childrens clothing and shoes at Sunset Presbyterian Church. No appointment needed. Hours: Wed. 9:30-11:30 a.m. and 4-6 p.m. Bus: 48

name: Old Town Closet (Old Town Church)
description: Free clothing, shoes, and small items for community members in need. Serves 100+ people each week, funded entirely by donations. Donations accepted by appointment only — email closet@oldtownfg.com. Eviction prevention support (Mercy program) also available through church.
hours: Tuesdays (clothing distribution). Call or email for hours.

name: Just Compassion Access Center offers a welcoming, low-barrier
description: space for people experiencing homelessness. Folks can grab a meal, take a shower, connect with providers and get help finding stable housing. Hours: 7 a.m.-7 p.m. daily. Bus:12, 78, 43
req: Must be experiencing homelessness or unstable housing

name: Project Homeless Connect
description: SE 6th Ave. Hillsboro, OR 97123 Breakfast and lunch served daily. Comfortable space for guests to spend time during the day. Cell phone charging stations, computer access, laundry, showers and food. Assistance with obtaining a state ID, housing resources and other services also available. Hours: Wed.-Sun. 9 a.m.-5 p.m. Bus: 57. Max: Blue

name: Domestic Violence Resource Center (DVRC)
description: (24/7 crisis line) Advocacy services for survivors of domestic violence. Advocate support available for filing protective orders, safety planning, resource referrals and more. Individual and group counseling for adults who have experienced intimate partner or domestic violence. Individual counseling for children and teens (ages 5-18) who have experienced and/or witnessed family violence. Servicios disponibles en español. All services are confidential and free of charge. Advocacy & Protective Orders: 503-640- 5352. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8:30 a.m.-noon, 12:30-4 p.m. Counsel- ing Services: 503-521-7997 Hours: Mon.-Thurs. 10 a.m.-7 p.m.
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: Family Justice Center
description: Wrap-around services for survivors of Domestic Violence, Sexual Assault, Sexual Exploitation, Elder Abuse, Family Violence and their children. Childcare, food, hygiene and legal support avail- able as well as referrals for housing, benefits and mental health support. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8:30 a.m.- 4 p.m
req: Free services — open to all genders unless specified

name: Kim’s Place
description: Confidential Location. Kim’s Place is a place of refuge, a sober living home that caters specifically to women who have left the life of the sex industry, including trafficked women. Our goal is to give each woman her dignity and the power over her own life back to her and to help her transition into her own home, a job and safety. Call or fill out online application at www.businessagainsttrafficking.com/kims- place/ to apply for a bed.
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: Monika’s House 1-866-469-8600
description: (24/7 crisis line) Confidential Location (Hillsboro). Provides safe, confidential emergency shelter and a domestic violence crisis hotline. Services for shelter residents include advocacy, case management and support groups. Call the crisis line to get connected
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: Oregon Crime Victims Law Center
description: Free legal information, referrals and representation for victims of crime regarding their crime victims’ rights in criminal cases; legal information and representation in protective order cases for survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault and stalking. Call for intake to determine eligibility for services. Serves all Oregon counties. Office visits by appointment only.

name: Sexual Assault Resource Center (SARC) 1-888-640-5311
description: (24/7 crisis line) Provides 24-hour support and response to survivors of sexual violence. Services are free and confidential to survivors of all genders. Emotional support, safety planning, resource refer- rals and more. Call or text the support line to get connected to services. Also provides education and training to raise aware- ness about sexual violence, connect survivors to resources and promote prevention
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: DVRC-Children and Youth Counseling
description: Individual counseling services for children and youth who have witnessed family violence. Individual counseling for teens 14 to 18 who have experienced teen dating violence. Bilingual (Spanish) services available. Call to schedule intake appt. Children and youth under 14 must have a non-offending parent or guardian willing to receive some parental education. Hours: Mon.-Thurs. 10 a.m.-7 p.m.
req: Serves families with children and parents/caregivers

name: Morrison Child and Family Services
address: 15455 NW Greenbrier Pkwy. Ste. 200 Beaverton, OR 97006
description: Specialized outpatient mental health services for children ages 2 through 18. Call for intake.
req: Serves families with children and parents/caregivers

name: Oregon Child Development Coalition (OCDC)
description: Early Childhood education for children from 6 weeks to 5 years old. Provides school readiness, family advocacy and engage- ment, health screenings and referrals, healthy meals and other services. Please call for more information
req: Serves families with children and parents/caregivers

name: Tigard-Tualatin Family Resource Center
description: Serves families with children ages 0–18 who live within the Tigard-Tualatin School District boundary. Basic needs resource connection, information on how to get clothing for babies and children, school supplies and early learning supports. Open on school days.

name: Women, Infants and Children (WIC)
description: Provides monthly healthy food benefits, growth monitoring and referral to other programs for pregnant and post-partum women, infants and children up to age 5. To make an appointment, call or submit an online interest form: https://forms.co.washington.or.us/form/wic-web-referral 254 N First Ave. Hillsboro, OR 97214 11515 SW Durham Rd. Ste. E8 Tigard, OR 97224
req: Pregnant, postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 (WIC)

name: Beaverton Resource Center
description: Central hub for the Beaverton community struggling with hun- ger, housing insecurity and poverty. Phone Hours: Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-2 p.m. Office hours: Tues., Wed. and Fri. 9 a.m.-1 p.m.

Insured or uninsured patients accepted. Financial assistance available. Hours: Mon. and Wed. 11 a.m.-7 p.m. Tues., Thurs., Fri. and Sat. 8 a.m.-4 p.m. Bus: 76, 78, MAX. www.ppcw.org

Bring all relevant documents to appointment

name: Oregon Law Center
address: 230 NE Second Ave. Ste. F Hillsboro, OR 97124
description: Serves low-income clients and seniors in Washington, Columbia, Clatsop, Tillamook and Yamhill Counties with civil legal services. No criminal or personal injury cases. Free legal assistance for low-income farmworkers, including wage claims, discrimina- tion and sexual harassment claims, housing and occupational health and safety. Call the general line for walk-in hours or for family law intakes. Public Benefits hotline 1-800-520-5292. English/Spanish. Provides legal services in the area of family law to low-income individuals and families in Washington, Yamhill and Columbia counties. Charges based on sliding scale, $50 for consultations via phone only. Request a consultation at https://www.salc- group.org/request
req: Sliding-scale fees based on income — free for those who cannot pay

name: Washington County Libraries
description: Washington County libraries are safe and welcoming places where you can cool off when it’s hot, or stay warm and dry when it’s wet outside. All 16 libraries across the county offer free wi-fi, public computers and printers. Libraries are open to the public- you do not need a library card to visit. If you would like to borrow books or access online learning resources, ask the staff about getting a free library card. Please visit wccls.org/libraries for additional locations and information. Hours vary by location. Beaverton City Library Hours: Mon.-Wed. 10 a.m. -7 p.m. Thurs.-Sat. 10 a.m.-6 p.m. Sun. 1-6 p.m. Hillsboro Public Library, Shute Park 775 SE 10th Ave. Hillsboro, OR 97123 Hours: Mon.-Thurs. 10 a.m. -8 p.m. Fri.-Sun. 10 a.m.-6 p.m.
