Hours: Mon. and Wed. 9 a.m.-4 a.m. Tues. and Thurs. 9 a.m.-6 p.m. Fri. 9 a.m.-3 a.m. Call for more info.

name: Sequoia Mental Health Services
description: Behavioral health and addiction recovery for adults, children, and families. No wait list for outpatient services. Accepts CareOregon, Trillium, and other Medicaid providers. Trauma-informed, culturally responsive care.

name: Vanguard Behavioral Health
description: Inpatient program for individuals with mental health and ad- diction challenges that offers therapy, medical and psychiatric care as well as peer support and case management. Call for availability.

name: Wash. County Behavioral Health- Crisis Line
address: 5250 NE Elam Young Parkway Ste. 100 Hillsboro, OR 97124
description: (24/7 crisis line) Crisis Line is answered 24 hours a day, seven days a week. Call if you or someone you care about is feeling depressed or anxious, having trouble with drug or alcohol use or thinking or talking about suicide

name: Wash. County Transition Age Youth Intensive Services
description: Transition Age Youth Intensive Services (TAYIS) supports youth ages 16-24 moving from the children’s mental health system to the adult system of care. Call 503-645-9010 for intake. Early Assessment and Support Alliance (EASA) supports adolescents and young adults ages 12-24 who are experiencing first symp- toms of a psychotic disorder. Call 503-705-

name: Washington County Behavioral Health Division
address: 5250 NE Elam Young Pkwy, Suite 700, Hillsboro, OR 97124
description: County mental health and addiction services hub. Crisis line answered 24/7. Walk-in available at Hawthorn Center. Free county-funded treatment available for uninsured with low income who meet criteria.
hours: Office: Mon–Fri 8:00 AM–5:00 PM | Crisis line: 24/7

name: Hawthorn Walk-In Center (Lifeworks NW)
description: Walk-in mental health and addiction crisis care for adults, youth, and children. Short-term stabilization and linkage to ongoing care. No appointment needed during walk-in hours. Crisis team can come to you.
hours: Walk-in: Mon–Fri 9:00 AM–2:00 PM | Crisis line: 24/7

name: NAMI Washington County
description: Free peer-led support groups, education, and advocacy for people with mental illness and their families. Partners with MHAAO for PeerZone workshops on mental health and addiction self-management.

name: Pacific University Psychology & Comprehensive Health Clinic
description: Psychological evaluation, counseling, and medication management. Serves children, adults, and families. Accepts OHP/Medicaid and sliding scale based on income. Assessments for learning disabilities, ADHD, cognitive issues.
req: Sliding-scale fees based on income — free for those who cannot pay

name: Problem Gambling Treatment (Oregon)
address: Washington County providers – call for location
description: Free and confidential treatment for problem gambling funded by Oregon lottery proceeds. Available to anyone, including family members. Call WA County Behavioral Health for local providers.
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: Community Action - Family Shelter
description: Shelter for families with children or mothers in the last trimester of pregnancy. Call to join the waitlist through Community Con- nect: 503-640-3263. Bus: 47, 57, MAX Blue. www.caowash.org Multiple Locations. Provides short-term shelter and case management for families with children (ages 0–18) and pregnant women. Each family is matched with a family advocate who helps create a personal- ized housing plan. Access through Community Connect referral system
req: Pregnant, postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 (WIC)
Waitlist may apply — call for current wait time

name: Open Door HousingWorks
address: 34420 SW Tualatin Valley Hwy. Hillsboro, OR 97123
description: Day shelter with meals, food boxes, showers, laundry, mail, and phone access (Mon.-Fri. 9 a.m.-3 p.m.). Housing support: shelter referrals, case management, transitional housing, and path to permanent housing. Cornelius Pod Village safe sleep site accessible via Community Connect. 34420 SW Tualatin Valley Hwy, Hillsboro. 503-640-6689. Bus: 57
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Project Homeless Connect Washington County
address: Hillsboro, OR (contact for access center location)
description: Shelter, street outreach, access center (showers, laundry, meals, resources), and housing case management. Tri-annual one-day resource events offering dental, haircuts, housing connections. Contact Community Connect for shelter waitlist.
req: Waitlist may apply — call for current wait time

name: Community Connect Homeless Response – Washington County
description: Central intake for shelter, safe sleep, housing, and rent assistance referrals. Front door to Washington County homeless system of care. Can also email CommunityConnect@CAOWash.org.
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Cornelius Pod Village (Conestoga Huts)
description: Safe sleeping pod shelter operated by Open Door HousingWorks. Funded by Washington County Homeless Services. Access via Community Connect.
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Day Center – Forest Grove (PHC)
address: 494 E Main Street, Forest Grove, OR 97116
description: Drop-in day center offering daytime safe space, resources, and basic services.
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Day Center – Beaverton
address: 34420 SW Tualatin Valley Hwy, Beaverton, OR
description: Drop-in day center with daytime safe space and community resources.
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Day Center – Tigard
description: Drop-in day center offering safe daytime space.
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: HIV Alliance
address: 12550 SW 2nd St. Beaverton (Enter on SE Washington Ave)
description: Mobile syringe exchange and harm reduction van that collects used syringes and distributes sterile syringes and injection sup- plies, Naloxone, fentanyl test strips, safer sex supplies, wound care supplies, hygiene supplies and other basic needs items. Beaverton WIC Clinic Mondays 1:30-3:30 p.m. Project Homeless Connect Wednesdays 11 a.m-1 p.m. 363 SE 6th Ave. Hillsboro, OR 97123 Hillsboro Bridge Shelter Wednesdays 2:30-4:30 p.m. 622 SE 10th Ave. Hillsboro, OR 97123

name: Disability, Aging and Veteran Services
address: 5240 NE Elam Young Pkwy. Ste. 300 Hillsboro, OR 97124
description: Empower older adults, veterans and people with disabilities by connecting them with essential services and resources to en- hance quality of life with compassion, dignity and respect. Help veterans and their families apply for federal benefits related to their service. We have information about benefits based on a veteran’s service in the armed forces. Hours: Mon.-Fri. 8 a.m.-5 p.m. The Salvation Army Veteran and Family Center 503-239-1259 14825 SW Farmington Rd. Beaverton, OR 97007 Grant per diem short-term transitional housing and case man- agement for veterans. Up to nine-month program for vets and their families. Emergency transitional housing for singles only up to 30 days with possible extension to 90 days maximum. VA
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member

name: Home Plate Youth Services
description: Beaverton Center: 12685 SW Fourth St. Hillsboro Center: 494 E Main St. Hillsboro Resource center helping young people ages 12-24 find hous- ing, education, employment, health referrals, clothes and food. Place to relax. Free meal. No religious doctrines. Laundry (at Beaverton location) and shower facilities. Beaverton Drop-In hours: Mon. 3-5 p.m. and 6-8 p.m., Tues. 2-5p.m., Wed. 3-5 p.m. and 6-8 p.m. Hillsboro Drop-In Hours: Thurs. 6-8 p.m. Northwest Regional Education Service District 503-614-1446 Provides free early intervention (ages 0-3) and early childhood special education (ages 3-5) services to children and families. Services include specially designed instruction and physical, occupational, or speech and language therapy. These services

name: Safe Place for Youth
description: Shelter, supportive services for homeless, runaway and at-risk youth ages 12 to 20. Food and basic needs assistance, case management and referrals. Up to 21-day emergency shelter program. Drop-in service hours: 9 a.m.-noon and 4-8 p.m. daily. School Homeless Liaisons Any student who is unsheltered, couch surfing or living in an insecure housing situation is eligible for homeless student ser- vices including transportation to school, immediate enrollment and connections to resources through The McKinney-Vento Act’s Education of Houseless Children and Youth Program. Phone
req: Call to confirm eligibility and current availability

name: Number by School District: Beaverton
req: Call to confirm eligibility and current availability

name: Second Home
description: Connects volunteer host homes with high school students 16 and older experiencing homelessness. Call or talk to school district homeless liaison for referral. Serves youth in Washington Clackamas, Lincoln and East Multnomah counties
req: Must be experiencing homelessness or unstable housing

name: 1998 SE Century Blvd. Hillsboro
description: ■Forest Grove High School 503-359-4057 1715 Nichols Lane Forest Grove ■ Tigard High School 503-431-5775 9000 SW Durham Rd. Tigard

name: Rose City Resource Guide, or to make updates or changes to a listing:
description: Email guide@streetroots.org or Call 503-210-5641 GENERAL QUESTIONS? Email guide@streetroots.org or call 503-210-5641 Thank you for your support. It takes all of us to keep this guide updated, trustworthy and accessible
req: Call to confirm eligibility and current availability

name: TriMet Honored Citizen Hop Card – Reduced Fare
address: Enrollment: 265 SE Oak St, Hillsboro, OR 97123
description: Reduced transit fare for low-income Oregon residents ages 18–64. $1.40 per 2.5 hrs or $28/month. Eligible if on OHP, SNAP, TANF, LIHEAP, or household income under 200% FPL. Bring proof of eligibility and photo ID.
hours: Hillsboro enrollment: Mon–Thu 8:30 AM–6 PM, Fri 8:30 AM–12:30 PM

name: Ride Connection – Free Door-to-Door Rides
description: Free door-to-door transportation for older adults and people with disabilities in Washington County. For medical appointments, meals, shopping, recreation, or work. Can help with bags. Community Connector buses also free and open to public.

name: Hillsboro Transportation Utility Fee Discount
description: Discount on city utility bill for households without a vehicle or those with an annual TriMet pass. Apply through City of Hillsboro utility billing.
req: May require Medicaid, disability documentation, or senior age (60+)

name: Oregon ONE Benefits Application (SNAP, OHP, TANF, ERDC)
address: Online or at local ODHS office
description: Single application for SNAP (food), OHP (health), TANF (cash), and ERDC (childcare). Apply online, by phone, or in person. No documents required to submit initial application.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: Washington County Benefits Enrollment Center
description: Free benefits enrollment help. Trained volunteers come to your home to help you apply for SNAP, Medicaid, energy assistance, property tax relief, prescription help, and Medicare savings programs. No cost.

name: PGE Low-Income Bill Discount (Oregon)
description: PGE customers may qualify for up to 60% monthly discount on energy use. Stackable with other assistance. Extended payment plans also available.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: NW Natural Bill Discount
address: NW Natural service area, Hillsboro OR
description: NW Natural gas customers may save 15–40% on monthly bills. Apply online.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: SNAP Training & Employment Program (STEP)
description: Free job coaching, career exploration, resume help, interview prep, and support services (transportation, tuition, work clothes) for SNAP/EBT recipients. Contact WorkSource Oregon to connect with a STEP Coach.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: Oregon Unemployment Insurance
description: Cash payments for people who lost jobs or had hours reduced through no fault of their own. Any worker authorized in the US may be eligible. Language interpretation available. Not considered public charge.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: Washington County WIC
description: Free healthy food, nutrition education, and breastfeeding support for pregnant individuals and families with children under 5.
req: Pregnant, postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 (WIC)

name: Centro Cultural – Prosperidad Program
description: Digital literacy and technology training for workforce. GED preparation for ages 16+. Free programs for Washington County residents.
req: Must be a Washington County resident

name: SkillUp Oregon – Free Online Training
description: Free virtual training platform powered by Metrix Learning. Helps Oregonians build skills, prepare for certifications, and expand career options. Launched by Oregon Workforce Partnership.
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Community Action WA County – Career Boost
description: Connects residents with career opportunities that lead to living wages. Workforce training and economic empowerment support.
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: WorkSource Oregon – Hillsboro
description: Free one-stop career center. Job search help, resume building, interview prep, skills training referrals, and STEP coaching for SNAP recipients. Partner of American Job Center network.
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Taste & See Local Food Bank
description: Community food bank accepting food, toiletries, diapers, formula, pet food, coats, sleeping bags, and blankets. Volunteers welcome Thursdays 9 a.m. until 2-3 p.m. Ask for Rochelle. Partner of Old Town Church.
hours: Donations accepted Wed. 8 a.m.-1 p.m. Volunteers: Thurs. 9 a.m.

name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
description: Free, confidential 24/7 support for people in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Call or text 988. Chat at 988lifeline.org. Veterans press 1. Spanish available.
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member
Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: Crisis Text Line
description: Free, 24/7 text-based crisis support. Text HOME to 741741 from anywhere in the USA. Trained crisis counselors respond. También disponible en español.

name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
description: 24/7 confidential support for domestic violence survivors. Call 1-800-799-SAFE, text START to 88788, or chat online. Safety planning, shelter referrals, legal resources. TTY: 1-800-787-3224.
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: RAINN National Sexual Assault Hotline
description: 24/7 free, confidential support for sexual assault survivors. Connects callers to local rape crisis centers. Online chat available.
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: National Human Trafficking Hotline
description: 24/7 support for trafficking survivors. Call 1-888-373-7888 or text HELP to 233733. Report tips, get local resources, crisis support. BeFree via text 233733.
req: Income eligibility typically required (under 125–200% federal poverty level)
Bring all relevant documents to appointment

name: Veterans Crisis Line
description: Free, confidential crisis support for veterans 24/7. Call 988 and press 1, text 838255, or chat online. For veterans, service members, National Guard, Reserve, and their families.
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member
Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: SAMHSA National Helpline
description: Free, confidential, 24/7 information and treatment referrals for mental health and substance use disorders. Español disponible. 1-800-662-HELP.
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI) Helpline
description: Free mental health information and referrals Mon-Fri 10 a.m.-10 p.m. ET. Text NAMI to 741741 for crisis support. Find local NAMI chapters for support groups.

name: 211 – Community Resource Helpline
description: Free, confidential nationwide helpline connecting people to local health and human services. Available 24/7 in most areas. Food, housing, health, crisis, and more. Also at 211.org.
req: Confidential services — information not shared without consent

name: findhelp.org (Social Services Search)
description: Free national database to search for local food, housing, health, and social services by ZIP code. Formerly Aunt Bertha. Find programs near you and apply online.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: Benefits.gov – Federal Benefits Finder
description: Official U.S. government website to screen for and find federal benefits programs. Search by state, life situation, or category — SNAP, Medicaid, housing, veterans, disability, and more.
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member

name: SNAP / Food Stamps (Federal)
description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program provides monthly food benefits loaded on an EBT card. Apply through your state's SNAP office or at prescreening.acf.hhs.gov. Eligibility based on income and household size.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: WIC – Women, Infants & Children (Federal)
description: Federal nutrition program for pregnant women, new mothers, infants, and children under 5. Provides food vouchers, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals. Apply at your local WIC office.
req: Pregnant, postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 (WIC)

name: Medicaid / CHIP Enrollment
description: Free or low-cost health coverage for low-income individuals and families. CHIP covers uninsured children. Apply anytime at healthcare.gov or through your state Medicaid office.

name: Medicare (Federal)
description: Federal health insurance for people 65+, and for people under 65 with certain disabilities. Covers hospital stays, doctor visits, prescriptions. 1-800-MEDICARE. TTY: 1-877-486-2048.

name: HealthCare.gov – ACA Insurance Marketplace
description: Find affordable health insurance plans through the Affordable Care Act marketplace. Income-based subsidies available. Open enrollment and special enrollment periods. Free enrollment assistance available.
req: Open to uninsured and low-income patients

name: Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program
description: Federal program providing HIV medical care, medications, and support services for low-income people with HIV. Find local Ryan White grantee programs through HRSA's HIV/AIDS Bureau.

name: Legal Services Corporation (LSC) – Legal Aid Finder
description: Find free civil legal aid in your area for low-income Americans. Issues include housing, family law, domestic violence, consumer debt, benefits, immigration. Find your local legal aid office at lsc.gov/find-legal-aid.

name: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Federal benefits for veterans and their families including healthcare, disability compensation, education (GI Bill), home loans, life insurance, pension, and burial benefits. Apply at va.gov or call 1-800-827-1000.
req: Must be a veteran or eligible family member

name: HUD – Housing Assistance Programs
description: Find public housing, Section 8 vouchers, homeless assistance, and housing counseling through HUD. Call 1-800-569-4287 for approved housing counselors. Find local public housing authorities at hud.gov.
req: Valid ID and income verification required

name: National Runaway Safeline
description: 24/7 crisis support for runaway and homeless youth and their families. Call 1-800-RUNAWAY, text 66008, or chat online. Resources, reunification help, local referrals.
req: Call to confirm eligibility and current availability

name: Childhelp National Child Abuse Hotline
description: 24/7 crisis intervention, information, and referrals for child abuse situations. Professional crisis counselors available. Call or text 1-800-4-A-CHILD.
req: Serves families with children and parents/caregivers

name: National Parent Helpline
description: Emotional support and resources for parents. Mon-Fri 10 a.m.-7 p.m. PT. Call 1-855-4A-PARENT. Trained advocates help parents deal with stress, family challenges, and crisis.
req: Serves families with children and parents/caregivers

name: National Low Income Housing Coalition
description: Advocacy organization tracking affordable housing policy. Find your state's housing needs, rent assistance programs, and emergency rental help resources at nlihc.org.
req: Documentation of housing need required (lease, eviction notice, or utility shutoff)

name: United Way Worldwide – 211
description: United Way partners with 211 across the country to provide referrals to local food pantries, rent assistance, utility help, health care, and more. Dial 211 or text your ZIP to 898-211.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: Feeding America – Food Bank Locator
description: Find your nearest food bank or food pantry in the Feeding America network. Over 200 food banks serving all 50 states and Puerto Rico. Search by ZIP at feedingamerica.org/find-your-local-foodbank.

name: No Kid Hungry – Free School Meals
description: All children ages 18 and under can get free meals at school year-round. Text FOOD to 304-304 to find nearby free meal sites. Summer food programs available when school is out.

name: Community Food Pantry Locator – FoodPantries.org
description: Directory of food pantries, soup kitchens, and food banks by state and city. Search for free food near you. Includes hours, addresses, and contact information.

name: National Coalition for the Homeless – Shelter Finder
description: Find local homeless shelters, transitional housing, soup kitchens, and services nationwide. Homelessshelterdirectory.org lists thousands of shelters by state and city.
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: TransLifeline
description: Peer support hotline run by and for transgender people. 24/7 support. Call 877-565-8860. No non-trans operators. Also offers micro-grants for name/gender marker changes.

name: Trevor Project (LGBTQ+ Youth)
description: 24/7 crisis intervention and suicide prevention for LGBTQ+ youth. Call 1-866-488-7386, text START to 678-678, or chat at TheTrevorProject.org.

name: GLBTQ Domestic Violence Project
description: Support services for LGBTQ+ survivors of domestic/sexual violence. Hotline: 800-832-1901. Bilingual services available.
req: Free services — open to all genders unless specified

name: Unemployment Insurance – CareerOneStop
description: Find your state's unemployment insurance program and file a claim. Also find job centers, training programs, resume help, and career exploration tools by ZIP code at careeronestop.org.
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Job Corps (Federal)
description: Free education and vocational training for young adults ages 16-24. Residential and non-residential programs nationwide. Earn GED/HSE, career certifications, and job placement support. Call 1-800-733-JOBS.

name: AmeriCorps VISTA – Anti-Poverty Programs
description: National service program placing volunteers with nonprofits fighting poverty. Find volunteer opportunities or connect with AmeriCorps-supported local nonprofits through americorps.gov.
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: National Disability Rights Network
description: Federally mandated Protection & Advocacy system in every state. Free legal help for people with disabilities: education, employment, housing, government benefits. Find your state P&A at ndrn.org.

name: Eldercare Locator
description: Nationwide resource connecting older adults and caregivers to local services. Transportation, meals, housing, home care, legal assistance. Mon-Fri 9 a.m.-8 p.m. ET. eldercare.acl.gov.
req: Serves adults 60+ and/or people with disabilities
Disability documentation may be required for some services

name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance (LIHEAP)
description: Federal program helping low-income households pay heating and cooling bills, repair furnaces, and weatherize homes. Apply through your state or local LIHEAP office. liheap.acf.hhs.gov.

name: Head Start / Early Head Start
description: Free comprehensive early childhood development for children birth-5 from low-income families. Includes education, health, nutrition, and parent engagement. Find local programs at eclkc.ohs.acf.hhs.gov.

name: National Domestic Workers Alliance
description: Support and resources for domestic workers (nannies, housecleaners, care workers). Know your rights, find legal help, join local chapters. domesticworkers.org.
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Immigration Legal Services – CLINIC
description: Catholic Legal Immigration Network. Find low-cost immigration legal services near you. Citizenship, DACA, family petitions, asylum, removal defense. clinic.org/find-immigration-legal-services.
req: Serves immigrants, refugees, and mixed-status families
Immigration status not required to access most services

name: National Immigration Law Center
description: Advocates for rights of low-income immigrants. Know Your Rights materials in multiple languages, policy analysis, and support for local legal aid organizations.
req: Serves immigrants, refugees, and mixed-status families

name: Planned Parenthood – Reproductive Health
description: Reproductive health services including birth control, STI testing/treatment, pregnancy testing, and more. Sliding-scale fees and Medicaid accepted at many locations. Find nearest center at plannedparenthood.org.
req: Insurance/Medicaid accepted; sliding scale for uninsured

name: GoodRx – Prescription Discount
description: Free prescription discount program. Save up to 80% on medications at 70,000+ pharmacies nationwide. No membership required. Use GoodRx coupon at checkout instead of insurance.
req: Open to uninsured and low-income patients

name: NeedyMeds – Patient Assistance Programs
description: Free database of patient assistance programs, drug discount programs, and disease-based assistance. Get free or discounted medications from pharmaceutical companies if you can't afford them.
req: Open to uninsured and low-income patients

name: National Reentry Resource Center
description: Resources for people returning from incarceration — housing, employment, benefits, legal aid, family reunification. Search by state at nationalreentryresourcecenter.org.
req: Serves people with criminal records or recently released

name: Prison Policy Initiative – Reentry Resources
description: Research and policy tools documenting mass incarceration. Find state-by-state reentry resources, voting rights restoration, driver's license reinstatement, and record expungement guides at prisonpolicy.org.
req: Serves people with criminal records or recently released

name: Modest Needs – Emergency Grants
description: Grants of $400-$1,000 for working-poor individuals facing small financial emergencies (medical bills, car repair, utilities). Apply at modestneeds.org. Also crowdfund your emergency at the same site.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: HRSA Health Center Finder
description: Find federally qualified health centers (FQHCs) that provide primary care regardless of ability to pay. Sliding-fee scale based on income. Dental, mental health, and substance use services also available at many sites.
req: Open to uninsured and low-income patients

name: Free Clinic Directory – NAFC
description: National Association of Free & Charitable Clinics. Find free and charitable clinics for uninsured and underinsured patients in your area. freeclinics.us.

name: Pet Food Stamps / Pet Pantry Network
description: Find pet food pantries and assistance programs near you. Petfoodpantry.org lists local pet food banks. Some Feeding America affiliates also provide pet food assistance.
req: Serves pet owners experiencing poverty or homelessness
Income or geographic eligibility may apply

name: ASPCA – Free & Low-Cost Vet Care
description: Find free or low-cost veterinary clinics, spay/neuter programs, and pet food assistance near you. aspca.org/pet-care/general-pet-care/low-cost-veterinary-care.
req: Serves pet owners experiencing poverty or homelessness
Income or geographic eligibility may apply

name: Alabama 211
description: Dial 211 for health and human services referrals statewide. Food, shelter, utility help, crisis support, and more. 24/7.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: Food Bank of North Alabama
description: Free food for food-insecure individuals in North Alabama. Partners with 200+ pantries, soup kitchens, and shelters. Find your nearest food pantry at foodbanknorthal.org.

name: Community Food Bank of Central Alabama
description: Free food assistance for 12 counties in Central Alabama. Food pantries, community kitchens, mobile pantries. Find locations at centralalabamafoodbank.org.

name: Foodbank of Southeast & South Central Alabama
description: Emergency food assistance for 19 counties in Southeast Alabama. Food pantry locator available at foodbankofse.org.

name: Alabama Department of Human Resources – SNAP
description: Apply for SNAP food benefits, Medicaid, CHIP, and cash assistance. Find your county DHR office at dhr.alabama.gov.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: Alabama Domestic Violence Hotline
description: 24/7 statewide DV hotline. Safety planning, emergency shelter referrals, legal advocacy. Also: National hotline 1-800-799-7233.
req: Free services — open to all genders unless specified

name: Legal Services Alabama
description: Free civil legal aid for low-income Alabamians. Housing, family law, consumer issues, benefits, DV. Find nearest office at legalservicesalabama.org.

name: Alabama Unemployment Insurance
description: File unemployment claims online or by phone. Benefits for eligible workers who lost jobs through no fault of their own. Reemployment services also available.
req: Generally open to adults seeking employment
Some programs have income or age eligibility

name: Alabama Housing Finance Authority
description: Down payment assistance, rental housing programs, emergency mortgage assistance. List of affordable housing. Homeless prevention resources.
req: Valid ID and income verification required

name: Alabama Mental Health Authority (ADMH)
description: State mental health system connecting residents to local mental health centers, crisis services, substance use treatment. Crisis line: 1-800-367-0955.

name: Salvation Army – Birmingham
description: Emergency food, utility assistance, rent help, shelter for families. Call for eligibility. Multiple metro Birmingham locations.
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space

name: Crisis Center Birmingham
description: 24/7 crisis intervention, mental health support, suicide prevention. Walk-in available. Serving Jefferson County.

name: UAB Medicine – Medically Indigent Services
description: Free or reduced-cost medical care for uninsured and low-income patients in Birmingham. Sliding fee scale. Specialty care available.
req: Sliding-scale fees based on income — free for those who cannot pay

name: Alaska 211
description: Dial 211 for local food, shelter, health, crisis, and social services statewide. Available 24/7.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: Food Bank of Alaska
description: Statewide food distribution network serving 350+ partner agencies. Food pantries, mobile distributions. Find partner agencies at foodbankofalaska.org.

name: Alaska Division of Public Assistance – SNAP/Medicaid
description: Apply for SNAP, Medicaid, CHIP, Temporary Assistance, and other benefits. myAlaska online portal or call your local DPA office.
req: Valid ID and proof of address/income typically required

name: Alaska Coalition on Housing and Homelessness
description: Statewide network connecting people to emergency shelter, transitional housing, and permanent supportive housing. Find local resources at alaskacoalition.org.
req: Call ahead to confirm availability and bed space
